extra.auc_note: deletion/insertion curves measured on the TRUE VCS by re-running the real ROM with top-saliency causes occluded (deletion) / restored (insertion); every point is a genuine emulator re-run, not a surrogate. NaN = a genuinely flat experiment (e.g. the vanishing position output).
extra.baseline: none (vanilla saliency uses no baseline and no input weighting)
extra.comparison_note: Vanilla saliency (|в€‚y/в€‚u|, bare gradient) is the baseline of the gradient family: GradГ—Input adds uвЉ™, DeepLIFT adds (uв€’baseline)вЉ™ + completeness, SmoothGrad adds noise-averaging. On the one-hot content read all share the same constant в€‚y/в€‚u=e_idx, so saliency already pins the self-byte; the siblings differ only in weighting/completeness, not in WHICH byte they top.

harness_positive_control.interpretation: corr=1 & precision@k=1 (on a non-degenerate column) в‡’ the scoring harness rewards a faithful map; the vanilla-saliency numbers are then a true measurement, not a harness artefact.

extra.method_ref: vanilla gradient / saliency (Simonyan et al. 2014): attribution = |в€‚y/в€‚u|
extra.output_note: CONTENT output: RAM byte 45 read one-hot through soft_ram_peek (в€‚y/в€‚ram[45]=1) вЂ” the most causally-active candidate concept byte the oracle found at this state. The STE gradient is ALIVE (grad L1=1.0), so vanilla saliency puts its mass on this self-byte (precision@1 vs the self-byte=1). Whether that equals the ORACLE's top cause is the FAITHFULNESS question: saliency answers 'what is y a function of NOW' (the self-byte), the oracle 'what CAUSES y over the horizon' (possibly an upstream byte) вЂ” measured corr=1.0, precision@3=1.0. Vanilla saliency has NO input weighting and NO completeness (unlike GradГ—Input/DeepLIFT) вЂ” the bare gradient baseline.
extra.scales_to_cluster_via: tools/cluster/xai_*.sbatch (E0-3) вЂ” batched SOFT-STE saliency over outputsГ—causesГ—games on GPU; the forward is bit-exact to this map.
extra.substrate: jutari (Julia) вЂ” Zygote content-path |в€‚y/в€‚u| over the RAM tape + TRUE-VCS deletion/insertion re-runs (real-ROM, bit-exact oracle machinery).

triad.S_note: held-out predictive fit: 16/16 held-out do(u) within Оµ=24.7 (calib=16, fit О”yв‰€239.0В·attr+0.0)
triad.definition: Fв€§Sв€§M triad (03_methods.tex sec:triad): F = faithfulness (runner-computed, unchanged); S = held-out predictive sufficiency over a disjoint do(u) split scored on the oracle's bit-exact re-runs; M = |U*|/|U_hat| (true minimal cause set / method-named set).
